behaviors.description: Cache keys generated from identical query parameters are always identical, and keys from different parameters never collide.
behaviors.justification: The core failure boundary is deterministic key-generation logic (normalization/serialization/hash), which is inside one local function/object and does not require I/O.
behaviors.test_description: Unit test the key-builder directly with table-driven and property-style inputs: same logical params in different orders/representations must produce identical keys; distinct param sets from a large representative corpus must produce distinct keys. Include edge cases (nulls, arrays, unicode, numeric/string forms).

behaviors.description: The TTL for a cache entry is computed correctly based on the data category's configured freshness rule (e.g., user profiles = 5 minutes, product catalog = 1 hour).
behaviors.justification: TTL mapping is business-rule computation from category/config to duration; the failure is local logic unless this behavior explicitly targets config loading infrastructure.
behaviors.test_description: Unit test `computeTTL(category, freshnessRules)` with fixed rule fixtures and a frozen clock if needed. Verify expected TTL per category, default/fallback behavior, and invalid category handling.

behaviors.description: When memory usage exceeds the configured threshold, the eviction strategy switches from LRU to LFU as specified in the configuration.
behaviors.justification: This failure typically appears at module handoff: memory-usage provider + config + strategy selector/cache manager. A pure unit of the selector can miss wiring/state-transition bugs.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test in-process modules wired together: inject controllable memory readings, set threshold/config, drive usage below then above threshold, and assert active strategy changes from LRU to LFU at the boundary (including hysteresis/flip-flop behavior if implemented).

behaviors.description: A write to the underlying PostgreSQL table invalidates all cache entries whose keys match the affected rows.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real PostgreSQL write behavior and DB-to-cache invalidation signaling (e.g., triggers/NOTIFY/listeners/CDC), which is often hidden by mocks and commonly fails only with a real DB process.
behaviors.test_description: System test with real PostgreSQL (container/local instance) plus running cache layer. Seed rows and corresponding cache keys, execute INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE on target table, then assert matching keys are invalidated and non-matching keys remain. Include timing/retry window for async invalidation propagation.

behaviors.description: The eviction policy removes the correct entries — least-recently-used under LRU, least-frequently-used under LFU — when the cache is full.
behaviors.justification: This is policy algorithm correctness on local cache state (recency/frequency bookkeeping and victim selection), testable without external systems.
behaviors.test_description: Unit test eviction engine with deterministic access sequences at fixed capacity. For LRU, verify oldest-by-recent-access is evicted; for LFU, verify lowest-frequency key is evicted (with explicit tie-breaker expectations). Assert post-eviction state and counters are correct.
